# Runa

Runa is a microkernel Go web framework for business applications. Core handles app startup, dependency injection, config, commands, host units, modules, lifecycle, and application time. HTTP route, cache, queue, database, storage, auth, sessions, views, i18n, observability, and drivers are installed as independent capability modules.

## Architecture

- Core: `github.com/duxweb/runa`; no HTTP router, cache, queue, database, or storage by default.
- Transports: `route`, `jsonrpc`, `ws`, and future `grpc` connect as host/capability modules.
- Capabilities: packages such as `cache`, `queue`, `database`, `storage`, `view`, `lang`, `task`, `event`, and `schedule` install with `app.Install(...)`.
- Drivers: external dependencies live in submodules such as `queue/redis`, `storage/s3`, and `database/oro`.
- Business code is organized with `provider.ModuleBase` modules. A module directory keeps only `module.go` at the root; admin/api/service/models/handler/command/listener/queue/schedule/middleware code belongs in subdirectories, matching the Dux module style.

## Naming rules

- Driver interfaces are named `Driver` inside their package.
- Driver selection options are named `Use(name)`.
- Provider options use `RegisterDriver(name, driver)` and `RegisterXxx(name, options...)`.
- Capability packages expose `New()`, `Provider(...)`, `Default()`, `Registry`, `types.go`, `options.go`, and `registry.go`.
- External driver subpackages expose a `Driver(...)` factory.
- Generated code should follow the naming conventions in `docs/src/content/docs/contributing/naming.mdx`.

## Common commands

- `runa dev --cmd serve`: rebuild and restart on Go/config changes.
- `runa gen module <name>`: generate a Dux-style business module with root `module.go` and subdirectories.
- `runa gen resource <name> --module <module>`: generate route resource handlers under `app/<module>/admin` by default.
- `runa gen crud <name> --module <module>`: generate CRUD builder and store skeleton under `app/<module>/admin` by default.
- `runa gen spec <file>`: generate modules and resources from a TOML spec.
- `runa gen provider <name>`: generate provider skeleton.
- `runa gen capability <name>`: generate a capability package.
- `runa gen driver <cap> <name>`: generate a capability driver submodule.

## Documentation map

- capabilities/asset: Asset — Install; Connect to an application; Standalone New usage; Config; Manifest; Use in templates
- capabilities/auth: Auth — Install; Connect to an application; Standalone New usage; Config; Authenticators; Permission checks
- capabilities/cache: Cache — Install; Connect to an application; Standalone New usage; Config; Redis driver; Common API
- capabilities/database: Database — Install; Connect to an application; Standalone New usage; Config; Oro driver; Redis driver
- capabilities/event: Event — Install; Connect to an application; Standalone New usage; Config; Listener options; Commands
- capabilities/index: Capabilities Overview — Connect capability blocks to an application; Standalone usage without the framework; Available capability blocks; Install external drivers on demand; Config files; Access capabilities in HTTP requests
- capabilities/lang: Lang — Install; Connect to an application; Message catalog layout; Translate in Go; HTTP language negotiation; Use t in templates
- capabilities/lock: Lock — Install; Connect to an application; Standalone New usage; Choosing Try, Wait, and With; Config; Drivers
- capabilities/log: Log — Install; Connect to an application; Standalone New usage; Config; Output types; Common channels
- capabilities/message: Message — Install; Connect to an application; Standalone New usage; Config; Driver integration; Topic matching
- capabilities/queue: Queue — Install; Connect to an application; Standalone New usage; Config; Redis driver; AMQP driver
- capabilities/rate: Rate Limit — Install; Connect to an application; Standalone New usage; Config; Drivers; Which algorithm to choose
- capabilities/rhtml: RHTML Templates — Install; Connect through view.Provider; Standalone usage; Configuration; Recommended layout; Layouts and blocks
- capabilities/schedule: Schedule — Install; Connect to an application; Standalone New usage; Config; Schedule options; Commands
- capabilities/session: Session — Install; Connect to an application; Standalone New usage; Config; Drivers; Common API
- capabilities/storage: Storage — Install; Connect to an application; Standalone New usage; Config; S3 driver; Common API
- capabilities/task: Task — Install; Connect to an application; Standalone New usage; Config; Dispatch options; Commands
- capabilities/view: View — Install; Connect to an application; Standalone New usage; Config; RHTML enhanced templates; Template sources
- contributing/architecture: Architecture Principles — Why micro-kernel; Architecture layers; What this means for users
- contributing/dependency-model: Dependency Model — Dependencies converge toward the kernel; How to decide module boundaries; Boundaries CI should enforce
- contributing/index: Contributing — How to start local development; Code contribution rules; What to check before submitting
- contributing/modules: Module Boundaries — Modules are split into four layers; Dependency directions that must not be broken; How to release nested modules; Shared cross-module code should not live in internal
- contributing/naming: Naming Conventions — Driver naming; Package file organization; Keep Provider shape consistent; Default only reads the default instance from DI; Avoid command name collisions
- contributing/testing: Testing — Test the root module first; Then run the full module matrix; Use go.work for local integration; Check whether the core accidentally imports optional modules; Run govulncheck by module
- contributing/write-capability: Write a Capability Block — Keep the directory clear first; Make New usable standalone first; Then provide a Default facade; Finally connect through Provider; Read config during Register; Acceptance checklist
- contributing/write-driver: Write a Driver — Where to put a driver module; Drivers only implement interfaces defined by capability packages; Register through the capability package; Join shutdown when holding connections; Acceptance checklist
- contributing/write-provider: Write a Provider — Start with a minimal Provider; Use it in an application; What each lifecycle stage should do; Register business modules; Register commands during Register; Acceptance checklist
- contributing/write-transport: Write a Transport Block — What a transport block is responsible for; Start with the minimum shape; Long-running services must use Host; Integrate with route only when HTTP is needed; Acceptance checklist
- core/app: Application Entry — Create an application; Install framework capabilities with Install; Register business domains with Module; Start the application; Use the global default application; Common mistakes
- core/command: Commands — Define a command; Register the command; Execute a command directly; Read arguments and write output; Command naming convention; Common questions
- core/config: Configuration — Where config files live; Minimal config example; Load config by environment; Split config by capability and module; Read config in code; Inspect config with a command
- core/di: Dependency Injection — Register dependencies inside Module; Read dependencies inside Module; Read dependencies after application startup; Provide and ProvideValue; When DI is safe to read; Read capabilities in HTTP requests
- core/index: Core Overview — How Runa is layered; What a kernel-only app looks like; Install route only when you need HTTP; Install capability packages when needed; Organize business code with Module; Why dependencies must be one-way
- core/lifecycle: Lifecycle — Application startup order; Init; Register; Boot; Shutdown; Host
- core/module: Business Modules — Module vs Install; Define a business module; Register the module; What can a Module register; Module can also shut down; Module dependency order
- core/timezone: Time and Timezone — Set the application timezone; Read current time; Common time helpers; Freeze time in tests; Do not measure duration with core.Now; Capabilities that follow app timezone
- drivers/crud-orostore: CRUD Oro Store — Install; Prerequisite; Define a model; Create CRUD APIs; Output transform; Writable fields
- drivers/database-oro: Database Oro — Install; Minimal setup; Config file; Multiple connections; Define an Oro model; Register models and sync schema
- drivers/index: Drivers and Adapters — How drivers connect; Current key drivers; Where to read about other drivers; Common mistakes
- extending/directory: Extension Directory — Official extensions; Choose by scenario; How to read this directory; Community extensions
- extending/index: Extension Ecosystem — What extensions usually provide; How to connect an extension; Extension vs business Module; Where to find extensions
- extending/middleware/audit: Audit Middleware — Install; Basic usage; Provider-managed config; Custom writer; Add audit fields; Skip requests

- extending/middleware/requestid: Request ID Middleware — Install; Basic usage; Read in a handler; Custom header and generator; Config fields; Input validation rules

- extending/middleware/session: Session Middleware — Install; Basic usage; Complete example; Read the current session; Config file; Use cache driver
